_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that we use different words and ways of talking depending on where we are and who we are with.

_source.w: We are learning that we use different words and ways of talking depending on where we are and who we are with.
y: Knowing how language changes in different situations helps us communicate well everywhere we go.
This helps us understand why we talk one way at home and another way at school.
When we match our language to the situation, people understand us better.
eg: We notice that we might ask a teacher for help differently from how we ask a friend.
We practise asking polite questions in class and compare with how we talk at the playground.
We role-play being at a shop and notice the language we use there.
We talk about how our voice might be louder at the park and quieter in the library.
We compare how we say 'hello' to a friend versus 'good morning' to a teacher.
We notice that we use different words with our family than with people we have just met.
